one_line_ask: Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready package: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, an outpainted banner, and an exported, packaged deliverable set.
brief:
Maple Cricket League (Backyard Cricket League Canada) is a streetwear-forward cricket league for the South Asian and Caribbean diaspora in the GTA, with an aesthetic in the lane of Rastah, OVO, Aimé Leon Dore, and IPL. The concept is fully developed; the client needs execution and production-ready, organized, named files.

You are the creative director executing the Adobe production-art thread. The client supplies: (a) a flat raster scan/mockup of the full logo system art (crest + monogram + hat badge laid out together) on a plain background; (b) an authored Illustrator master file (.ai) of the same three marks set up on three artboards, ready to export; (c) a hero brand photo of a street cricket scene shot in mixed/overcast GTA light; (d) a studio-style photo of a folded home jersey on a plain seamless backdrop for the e-commerce/lookbook cut-out; (e) a flat photo of the away-kit chest crest graphic to be turned into a single-color screen-print/sublimation separation.

Produce, by chaining Adobe connector tools: (1) a clean, scalable VECTOR of the logo system from the raster scan (raster→SVG), then an exported vector master from the supplied authored .ai for the manufacturer; (2)
